Hey Grant Cardone here, and I want to talk to the Gen Y's out there I just did an interview with Forbes magazine and the question was the gentleman said I have one question for you I said okay, what is it? He's like no no no I got one question. I only need one thing from you What should 17 million Gen Y's do to get a job and this was my answer Gen Y, okay? Take your resume tear it up Throw it in the trash number one number two I'm gonna give you more than one thing number two do not talk to the HR department to get a job You won't get one like that number three I want you to make a list of three to five people you want to work for do disregard the industry you want to be in okay? You want to work in an industry good now? I want you to get down to three to five people Target exactly who owns that business and I want you to do the best sales presentation of your lifetime When you get in front of those three to five people that run that business that own that business a guy like me I want you to sit down with that guy tear up the resume Because what he is gonna hire who you are is in that chair You need to look that guy dead in the eyes and tell him or her Why you want to work for him? Why you are so passionate so excited about working for this individual and that you'll put your life on the line to work with him That you're there to dedicate your life to him. Oh my gosh I don't want to dedicate my life Dude you're gonna dedicate your life to somebody you either gonna dedicate your life to being great with some other company Or you're gonna dedicate your life to looking for the perfect situation for a long time Gen Y Get serious get serious right now tear up the resume Disregard the HR department and number three what are you gonna do? You're gonna get three to five people that you want to work for I want to work for Grant Cardone good Then you figure out how to get a meeting with me across from my desk Look me dead in the eye and say dude.
